ingredients: 4 mackerel fillets, pin-boned, each cut into 4 large pieces (or other firm fish fillets)
Thumb-size piece fresh ginger, finely chopped
1 long dried red chilli (optional), cut into pieces
1 tsp cornflour mixed with 1 tbsp cold water
Salt and black pepper, to taste
steps: Make sauce base: mix dark soy, rice vinegar, ketchup, fish sauce and about 100 ml water.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a wok/pan; stir-fry onion, garlic and ginger briefly. Add sauce base, then pineapple and chilli and simmer 3-4 minutes.
Stir in cornflour slurry and cook until thick and glossy; season with black pepper.
Coat fish pieces in flour with a pinch of salt. Fry in hot oil until golden and crisp, 1-2 minutes per side; drain.
Reheat sauce until bubbling, spoon into plates, top with fish and scatter spring onion.
tips: Use a firm fish that can handle frying; keep the sauce hot and thick so the fish stays crisp as long as possible.
